title: Rapamycin + Trametinib Combo Extends Mouse Lifespan 35% (Female) and 27% (Male)
description: The biggest single longevity result of the week: combining two FDA-approved drugs (rapamycin + trametinib) extended median mouse lifespan by 34.9% in females and 27.4% in males, with max lifespan up 32% and 26% respectively. The combo also delayed tumor growth, preserved heart function, and reduced inflammation—though testicular degeneration and fatty liver were observed, calling for human dose-finding work.

title: Oxford Aging Cell: Rapamycin Cuts T-Cell DNA Damage in Humans, Doubles Survival in Vitro
description: An Oxford study published in Aging Cell shows 1 mg/day oral rapamycin for 4 months in older men reduced T-cell DNA damage, mTOR hyperactivation, and cellular senescence markers, while doubling T-cell survival under stress in vitro. This is one of the clearest human-tissue mechanism readouts yet for rapamycin as an immune rejuvenator and supports the growing low-dose human protocol movement.

title: Stanford: 15-PGDH Inhibition Regrows Cartilage in Old Mice and Human Tissue
description: Stanford Medicine reports that blocking the gerozyme 15-PGDH regrew lost knee cartilage in aged mice and triggered new cartilage formation in human samples collected during knee replacements. An oral version is already in clinical trials for muscle weakness, opening the door to a drug that could one day replace many knee and hip replacement surgeries.

title: Palvella Submits First Rolling NDA Module for QTORIN Topical Rapamycin
description: Palvella Therapeutics (Nasdaq: PVLA) submitted the first module of its rolling NDA to the FDA for QTORIN 3.9% rapamycin anhydrous gel for microcystic lymphatic malformations, on track to complete the filing in H2 2026. If approved, QTORIN would be the first FDA-approved topical rapamycin and a regulatory template for the broader topical mTOR-modulator longevity dermatology category.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Extends Remaining Lifespan of Middle-Aged Mice by 34%
description: Seragon Biosciences announced that its lead longevity candidate SRN-901—shares technology lineage with RESTORIN—extended the remaining lifespan of middle-aged mice by ≈34% in one of the most comprehensive multi-year preclinical studies to date. The result reportedly outperforms rapamycin on lifespan extension and accelerates Seragon's path toward human translation.

title: FDA-Approved Rapamycin + Trametinib Combo Extends Mouse Lifespan >30%
description: Combo outperforms either drug alone with 35% female / 27% male median lifespan gain and tumor + cardiac benefits; some testicular and liver side effects noted.

title: Oxford: Rapamycin Reduces T-Cell DNA Damage and Doubles Survival
description: Aging Cell study shows 1 mg/day rapamycin in older men cuts T-cell DNA damage and senescence — direct human-tissue evidence for immune rejuvenation.

title: Stanford 15-PGDH Inhibitor Regrows Knee Cartilage in Aged Mice
description: Blocking the gerozyme 15-PGDH restored lost cartilage in old mice and produced new cartilage in human knee-replacement tissue; oral version already in muscle-weakness trials.

title: Palvella Submits First QTORIN Rapamycin NDA Module to FDA
description: Rolling NDA for topical 3.9% rapamycin anhydrous gel for microcystic lymphatic malformations targets H2 2026 completion — first FDA-approved topical rapamycin would set the regulatory pathway.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Boosts Remaining Lifespan of Middle-Aged Mice 34%
description: Multi-year preclinical study reports SRN-901 outperforms rapamycin on lifespan extension; full results to publish in coming months.

title: Rhodiola rosea Confirmed as Natural Senolytic in Multiple Mouse Models
description: Juntendo iScience study: Rosea extract counters hair loss, graying, skin decline, and improves metabolic health by clearing senescent cells.

title: Senolytic D+Q Phase 1 Confirms Safety in Alzheimer's Patients
description: Dasatinib + quercetin trial shows safety but no cognition or MRI effects at small N; biomarker pattern suggests senescent cells were killed.

title: Topical ABT-263 Rejuvenates Aged Skin and Speeds Wound Healing
description: 5-day topical senolytic ABT-263 cleared p16/p21 senescence markers and pushed 80% of aged mice to full wound closure by day 24 versus controls.

title: Sentcell + UCL Launch First-In-Human T-Cell Rejuvenation Trial
description: Phase 1 trial led by Dr. Alessio Lanna at UCL will test an injectable therapy from Sentcell designed to reverse T-cell senescence in adults with immune dysfunction.

title: Stanfield + Kaeberlein Rapamycin Strength Trial: 40 Older Adults, 13 Weeks
description: Double-blind 6 mg/week rapamycin RCT tests muscle strength, endurance, biological age, and inflammation markers in adults 65-80 — readout expected late 2026.

title: Retro Biosciences RTR242 Autophagy Drug Enters Phase 1 in Australia
description: Orally bioavailable, blood-brain-barrier-penetrant autophagy enhancer is mapping safety and MTD; Retro's three concurrent pipelines include reprogramming and plasma-inspired therapeutics.

title: Nature Aging Paper Profiles D+Q Tissue-Specific Mechanisms
description: Integrative transcriptomics + single-cell + histopathology show D+Q remodels immune function and tissue inflammation; early + prolonged dosing amplifies efficacy.

title: Nature Aging: Hierarchical Endpoints and Win Statistics for Geromedicine Trials
description: Statistical framework paper outlines how composite hierarchical endpoints could make multi-system geroprotector trials feasible at smaller N.

title: Senolytics Reduce Age-Related Bone Loss in Mice
description: D+Q senolytic combination cut alveolar bone loss and root exposure in aged mice — bone-targeted senolytic mechanism confirmed.

title: Modern Aesthetics: mTORC1-Selective Topicals (RLX-201) Move Forward
description: Article surveys next-gen topical mTOR-modulators that selectively inhibit mTORC1 while preserving mTORC2 to protect skin barrier — Rapalogix RLX-201 highlighted.

title: Bryan Johnson's Top 3 Anti-Aging Compounds: Rapamycin, Taurine, Acarbose
description: Johnson pairs rapamycin + metformin + olive-oil bioavailability vehicle; monitors blood sirolimus level, body temp, metabolic panel.

title: Kaeberlein Supplement Stack: Rapamycin, Vit D, Omega-3, Prebiotics, Magnesium
description: Cyclic 12-week-on / 6-12-month-off rapamycin pattern; explicitly skeptical of NAD+ precursors absent better evidence.

title: David Sinclair Confirms On-and-Off Rapamycin Use; Diamandis at 6 mg/wk
description: Abundance360 interview disclosure positions cyclic 6 mg-class rapamycin dosing as a mainstream physician-investor protocol.

title: Time-Restricted Eating Mitigates Muscle Aging — Oxford Nutrition Reviews
description: Meta-analysis links TRF/TRE to better mitochondrial function, autophagy, AMPK/mTOR signaling — a low-cost protocol with growing evidence.

title: Fight Aging Newsletter June 29 — Epigenetic Reprogramming, Senescence, Trial Updates
description: Weekly digest synthesizes Sentcell T-cell trial launch, diverse interventions reducing epigenetic age, and the broadening senolytic + reprogramming pipeline.

title: "Zombie Cells" Aren't Always Bad — Senescence Has Beneficial Roles
description: Recent research nuances senescence biology by identifying contexts where senescent cells aid wound healing and embryogenesis — calls for selective rather than blanket senolytics.

title: New Drugs Target Senescent Cells to Improve Cancer Outcomes
description: Three mouse cancer models show new senolytic agents reduce tumor size and extend survival — confirms cross-domain senescence drug strategy.

title: Nasdaq: PVLA Among 6 Biotech Stocks With Massive 2026 Gains
description: Palvella (+500%) makes the year's biggest biotech-mover list ahead of QTORIN rapamycin NDA completion — institutional investor flow into longevity-adjacent biotech accelerates.

title: 1440: Trametinib + Rapamycin Drug Combination Extends Mouse Lifespan
description: Mainstream newsletter coverage highlights the rapamycin + trametinib combo, broadening public awareness of additive longevity pharmacology.

title: Bioworld In the Clinic Roundup — June 24, 2026
description: Industry digest enumerates this week's longevity-adjacent INDs, Phase 1 starts, and readouts across multiple geroscience platforms.

title: Brain-Aging Protein Identified With Path to Inhibition
description: Re-surfaced study identifies a specific protein driving brain aging and outlines an inhibition strategy now being explored by multiple labs.
